paper_id: Buchtela and Vynikarova - 2020 - Connection between ontouml and knowledge representation model of students' activities
input_limitations: The extracted text contains encoding noise in some characters and symbols, but the main sections and relevant purpose statements are readable.

models.model_label: Knowledge Representation Model of Students' Activities (KRMSA) for a Moodle course model situation

evidence_quotes.quote: The students' way through an electronic course (e.g. realized in LMS Moodle) can be described using the Knowledge Representation Model of Students' Activity (KRMSA).

This article proposes a Knowledge Representation Model of Students' Activities (KRMSA), which describes a model situation of a student passing an electronic course and obtaining credit from this course.

goals.justification: The quoted statements frame KRMSA as a model for describing the student's way through an electronic course. This supports domain understanding, though the paper uses description/representation language rather than explicitly saying the model was made for shared understanding or meaning negotiation.

evidence_quotes.quote: The resulting model in OntoUML is relatively easily convertible to an implementation model (Rybola, 2016) and can thus be the cornerstone of applications in the background of the primary LMS or applications of subject domain ontologies (Stoyanova-Doycheva, 2019).

From the conceptual model in OntoUML, it is then possible to derive a specific implementation model usable, for example, for automatic student evaluation in a given course or as the basis of decision support systems in the background of LMS Moodle.

goals.justification: The paper frames the OntoUML part of KRMSA as transformable into an implementation model and usable for automatic evaluation or LMS background systems. This is implementation-oriented intended use, but confidence is Medium because the paper presents a hypothetical model situation rather than an actually deployed implementation.

evidence_quotes.quote: From the conceptual model in OntoUML, it is then possible to derive a specific implementation model usable, for example, for automatic student evaluation in a given course or as the basis of decision support systems in the background of LMS Moodle.

uncertain_goals.reason: The mention of decision support systems suggests possible decision support, but the quoted use is framed mainly as deriving an implementation model, so the evidence is too weak to code Problem-solving as present.
